prompt: Write a Rust function `fn add(a: i32, b: i32) -> i32` that returns the sum. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
Write a Rust function `fn is_prime(n: u64) -> bool` that returns whether n is prime (n < 2 is not prime). Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
test: fn main() { assert!(is_prime(2)); assert!(is_prime(97)); assert!(!is_prime(1)); assert!(!is_prime(15)); }

prompt: Write a Rust function `fn fib(n: u32) -> u64` returning the n-th Fibonacci number with fib(0)=0, fib(1)=1. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
Write a Rust function `fn reverse_words(s: &str) -> String` that reverses the order of space-separated words. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
test: fn main() { assert_eq!(reverse_words("hello world"), "world hello"); assert_eq!(reverse_words("a b c"), "c b a"); }

prompt: Write a Rust function `fn gcd(a: u64, b: u64) -> u64` returning the greatest common divisor. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.

Write a Rust function `fn is_palindrome(s: &str) -> bool` that returns whether the string reads the same forwards and backwards considering only ascii alphanumeric characters, case-insensitive. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
test: fn main() { assert!(is_palindrome("A man, a plan, a canal: Panama")); assert!(!is_palindrome("hello")); assert!(is_palindrome("")); }

prompt: Write a Rust function `fn max_subarray(nums: &[i64]) -> i64` returning the maximum sum of any contiguous non-empty subarray. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.

Write a Rust function `fn two_sum(nums: &[i32], target: i32) -> (usize, usize)` returning the two indices (i, j) with i < j such that nums[i]+nums[j]==target. Assume exactly one solution. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
Write a Rust function `fn merge_sorted(a: &[i32], b: &[i32]) -> Vec<i32>` merging two ascending-sorted slices into one sorted Vec. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.

Write a Rust function `fn binary_search(arr: &[i32], target: i32) -> i64` returning the index of target in a sorted ascending slice, or -1 if absent. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.

Write a Rust function `fn word_count(text: &str) -> std::collections::HashMap<String, usize>` mapping each whitespace-separated word to its count. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
test: fn main() { let m = word_count("a b a"); assert_eq!(m["a"],2); assert_eq!(m["b"],1); assert!(word_count("").is_empty()); }

prompt: Write a Rust function `fn to_roman(n: u32) -> String` converting an integer 1..=3999 to a Roman numeral. Return only the function in a ```rust code block, no main.
test: fn main() { assert_eq!(to_roman(4),"IV"); assert_eq!(to_roman(9),"IX"); assert_eq!(to_roman(58),"LVIII"); assert_eq!(to_roman(1994),"MCMXCIV"); }
